Provisions of the “Yessenov Data Lab” Summer School Program of the Shakhmardan Yessenov Science and Education Foundation 1. General provisions 1.1. Yessenov Data Lab Summer School program (hereinafter referred to as the "Program") is a 5-week intensive training of Kazakhstan's young scientists in basic skills of volume data analysis for further application of knowledge in real practical work, as well as the development of their communication skills 1.2. Тһе purpose of the program is to develop the skills of young scientists for working with large volumes of data focused on teaching the basics of mathematics, necessary for further independent development in this direction. It also implies the development of non-academic (communicative) skills and leadership qualities of young scientists. 1.3. Іп these Provisions, the term “sponsorship” is equivalent to a financial support provided to participants residing outside of Almaty. For the duration of the Program, the sponsorship covers a two-way domestic flight to Almaty, accommodation and daily meals (on ground or a cash equivalent of the listed expenses). Participation in the Program is free of charge for all winners of the Program.

Twenty-five (25) winners are selected annually amongst all competitors. The number of winners is subject to the quantity and the quality of applications and can be increased or decreased. 1.4. The winners are selected in an open competition held at least once a year. 1.5. The Program foresees a 5-week Data Science training from June 15, 2020 until July 17, 2020. The language of training is Russian, with the occasional usage of technical English. 2. Participation requirements 2.1. In order to take part in the Program one has to be a citizen of the Republic of Kazakhstan (over 18), а student (undergraduate, graduate or Ph.D.) or practicing specialist. 2.2. Candidates must demonstrate high intellectual capacity, possess a wide range of professional skills, have commitment and be result-oriented, single-minded and flexible. 2.3. Candidates must demonstrate their active professional, scientific, and social activities, and the conformity of their professional skills with the following requirements: Requirements to candidates: e Strong analytical skills e Basic knowledge of statistics and linear algebra e Тһе following is a plus: o Programming skills; o Knowledge of source control

(git/svn/...), command line/ssh, testing, developer methodologies (Agile/spiral/...), cloud familiarity (AW S/Azure/...); о Certificates of successful completion of programming courses or a bachelor diploma in Science in CS or other tech discipline (math, physics, and engineering); o Upper intermediate English (6.5 IELTS or 90 TOEFL ibt or other way to show fluency). 3. Competition stages 3.1. Тһе competition has three (3) rounds. Applicants that demonstrated the best skills are selected upon the completion of each round. The competition is announced on the foundation’s website yessenovfoundation.org and its social networks. 3.2. First round: applications’ submission and assessment. Candidates submit their applications from 24 February to 15 March 2020. The following documents must be included in the application: 1. — А сору of the candidate’s national ID; 2. Асору of the candidate’s transcript (all completed semesters) and a copy of Bachelor degree diploma with transcript (for graduates and specialists); 3. Ап essay on the following topic “I want to study data analysis to...” (at least 7 sentences and not more than 2 pages); as A portfolio indicating the candidate's achievements

in research and/or expressed interests in a particular scientific field with respective evidence: — омп scientific articles, materials, results of any research, etc. ; 6. Certificate demonstrating the candidate’s good command of the English language (if any). An application with the entire package of documents must be submitted by filling out an online application form on the Foundation's website - yessenovfoundation.org A candidate’s submission of documents indicates the candidate compliance to the Program objectives and confirms his/her acceptance of and readiness to comply with the conditions of the competition described herein. Candidates can submit their documents in Kazakh, English or Russian. All applications are registered by an employee of the foundation, and a personal registration number is assigned to each candidate. Upon the verification of the completeness and the conformity of a candidate’s application to the formal criteria of the Program, a decision is rendered on whether the candidate is accepted or rejected. An acceptance/rejection notice is solely sent to the email address specified in a candidate’s application form. Russian is the Program’s working language.

All duly submitted applications are reviewed and assessed by the foundation’s employees and independent experts in the period of 16-30 March 2020. The list of the first round’s successful candidates is posted on the foundation’s website. No more than 60 candidates are selected for the second round. 3.3. Second round: examination 1. Mathematics and logic testing The test is composed of 30 question (60 minutes), and must be completed online; 2. A Data Science test on the Kaggle platform. A simple regression task will be given, and the participants will be ranked by the root-mean-square error; 3. А psychometric test. The second round is held on 31 March to 15 April 2020. The examination is intended to assess each candidate’s analytical skills. The examination requires basic knowledge in: 1. Matrixes, simple equations 2. Columns (math) . Linear regression, mean values, medians . Producing function graphs . Variables, cycles, conditions (in any programming language) . Massifs, sorting . Algorithms on columns (programming) . Basic knowledge of statistics and probability theory — probability distribution, random value, math expectation, covariance, normal distribution ANDOaABRW The list

of successful second round candidates is posted on the foundation’s website. No more than 40 candidates are selected for the third round. 3.4 Third round: interviews The third round is held on 16-22 April 2020. The participants of the third round will be invited for a face-to-face or online interview with the experts. The following competencies are considered during the interview: е =Analytical skills; e Professional skills; e Programming skills and experience (if possible); e Personal qualities; e Other. The following qualities are also considered in the selection: e high intellectual capacity; e eagerness to continually develop their professional skills; e valuable contribution to social life, etc. Тһе Foundation takes а final decision before 30 Ари 2020. The list of winners of the competition is published on the Foundation's website. The results of the third round are reported to the winners by e-mail. 3.5 A council of experts is formed to select the winners of the competition, which will select candidates on the base of the results of all three competitive rounds described above. The decision of the experts is submitted to be reviewed by the Board of Trustees of the Foundation.

3.6 The decision of the Board of Trustees is approved by the Foundation in the form of an officially certified list of the Program’s winners. 4 Sponsorship payment procedure 4.1. The sponsorship is awarded to twenty-five (25) winners shortlisted in the course of the competition. The number of winners is subject to the quantity and the quality of the applications and can be increased or decreased. 4.2. Following the results of the competition, the winners will undertake a 5-week long training on a gratuitous basis at the Yessenov Data Lab summer school from 15 June to 17 July 2020. The dates of the training may change, without exceeding 14 calendar days. The language of training is Russian with occasional usage of technical English. 4.3 For the participant residing outside of Almaty, the Foundation will cover a two-way domestic flight to Almaty, accommodation and daily meals (on ground or a cash equivalent of the listed expenses) for the period of the Program. The foundation reserves the right of final say in the choice of accommodation for the winners receiving the sponsorship. 4.4 A sponsorship agreement is signed between the Foundation and the Program winner before the start of

the “Yessenov Data Lab” summer school training. The agreement may also provide for other arrangements made between the Program winner and the Foundation. 4.5 The Foundation may provide other privileges upon further agreement, including online training, involvement in conferences, business meetings, etc. 5. Additional information. PROVISIONS of the Yessenov Data Lab Program SHAKHMARDAN YESSENOV FOUNDATION 1. General terms. 1.1 The Yessenov Data Lab Program (hereinafter referred to as “the Program’) is а 4-week intensive training for Kazakhstani young scientists/professionals in basic Big Data analysis skills to apply knowledge to further work. 1.2. The program is funded by the initiative of the private “The Shakhmardan Yessenov Science & Education Foundation” (hereinafter referred to as “the Foundation’). 1.3. The Program is aimed to develop the skills of young scientists to work with Big Data which will encourage them to independently develop in this field. 1.4. In these Provisions, the term “sponsorship” implies the funding for training, including payment of fees for lecturers, technical and administrative costs. 1.5. Participation in the Program is free of charge for the winners. 20 (twenty) Program winners are selected from among the Program contestants annually. 1.6. The winners of the Program are determined in an open contest. The contest is held once a year. 1.7. The program involves training in Big Data skills for 4 (four) weeks from June 17 to July 13, 2024, at the Yessenov Data Lab Foundation

Summer School. This includes 3 (three) weeks online - from June 17 to July 5, 2024, and 1 (one) week offline - from July 8 through July 13, 2024, in Almaty. Language of instruction is Russian, with possible use of elements of technical English. |. Requirements for the Program participants. 2.1. Only citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan who are over 18 (eighteen) years of age, undergraduate students, master’s students, doctoral students and working professionals in the following areas* can participate in the Program: - natural sciences, mathematics and statistics; - information and communication technologies; - engineering, manufacturing and construction industries; - healthcare. * according to the Classifier of Training Programs for Personnel with Higher and Post-Graduate Education (Approved by the Order of the Minister of Education and Science of the Republic of Kazakhstan as of October 13, 2018, No. 569). 2.2. The following qualities of applicants will be taken into consideration when determining the winner of the Program: 2.2.1. high intellectual potential; 2.2.2. willingness to develop professional skills; 2.2.3. focusing on results; 2.2.4. adaptability. 2.3. The applicants

are to declare and document their active professional, scientific, social activities and compliance of professional skills with the following requirements: 2.3.1. strong analytical skills; 2.3.2. interest and willingness to engage in scientific activities and/or develop IT technologies in Kazakhstan; 2.3.3. basic knowledge of statistics and linear algebra; 2.3.4. basic knowledge of programming (in any programming language). 2.4. The applicant must have a personal laptop with characteristics sufficient for comfortable programming. Recommended parameters: 2.4.1. CPU Intel Core Gen 7 and higher, Mac Silicon M1 and higher. 2.4.2. RAM 6 GB and higher. 2.5. To have a stable Internet connection with a recommended speed of 20 Mbps, but not less than 10 Mbps, or higher. 2.6 The following will be an advantage: 2.6.1 interest in science; 2.6.2 own scientific papers, materials, results of any research, etc.; 2.6.3 B1 or higher proficiency in English. Ш. Stages of competitive selection process of the Program. 3.1. The contest implies 3 (three) selection stages, at the end of each of which the best applicants are selected. The announcement about the opening of the contest is published on the

Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org and on the Foundation’s social media. 3.2. To hold the contest, the Foundation creates an Expert Council, which selects participants according to the results of 3 (three) rounds of the Program contest, as described below. 3.3. The decision of the Expert Council of Trustees is approved by the Foundation’s order which contains officially certified list of Program winners. The results of the rounds and the list of winners of the Competition are published on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org. 3.4. The first round is acceptance and selection of applications. 3.4.1. Applications are accepted on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org, from March 14 through April 17, 2024. The deadline for acceptance of applications is April 17, 2024, 3:00 P.M. Astana time (GMT+5). Applications received after the deadline will not be considered. The Applicant must attach to the application: Mandatory documents: 1. Copy of ID; 2. A copy of the transcript (all semesters) or undergraduate diploma with grades (for undergraduates and professionals); 3. An essay on the topic “I want to learn how to analyze data so that...” (at least 7

sentences and no more than 750 words); 4. СУ Additional documents: 5. A portfolio demonstrating the candidate’s achievements in scientific research and/or evident interest in a particular scientific area, with the relevant confirmation: 6. completion of the course - no more than 2 (two) certificates’; 7. publications — no more than 2 (two) publications*; 8. certificates in science - no more than 2 (two) certificates”; 9. own scientific papers, materials, results of any research; 10. Certificate confirming English language proficiency (if available). *The Foundation considers a limited number of certificates. The candidate must independently select the certificates that best represent personal achievements. 3.4.2. An application and all the documents must be submitted by filling out an online application form on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org. 3.4.3. An application and essay can be submitted in the Kazakh or Russian languages. Documents (certificates, diplomas, recommendations, etc.) can be provided in their original languages (Kazakh or Russian, or English). 3.4.4. Submission documents to the contest means that the applicant shares the goals of the Program and

agrees with all the terms of the contest set out in these Provisions and is ready to fully comply with them. 3.4.5. All the applications received on time are reviewed and assessed by the Foundation employees and independent experts from April 18 through May 8, 2024. 3.4.6. Lists of the applicants who advanced to the second round are published on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org. Upon completion of the first round of the contest, no more than 60 (sixty) candidates are selected. 3.5. The second round is testing. 3.5.1. The second round is held from May 6 through May 10, 2024. 3.5.1. Тһе Maths and Logic test. Тһе test consists of 30 (thirty) questions and lasts 60 minutes. It is held online; 3.5.2. Data analysis exam. The exam is a regression problem. 3.5.3. For a successful passing the test, the candidate must have basic knowledge of the following topics: 1) matrices, linear equations; 2) graphs (mathematics); 3) linear regression, means, medians; 4) graphing functions; 5) variables, loops, conditions, arrays (in any programming language); 6) basic knowledge of statistics and probability theory - probability distributions, random variable, math. expectation,

covariance, normal distribution. 3.5.4. Lists of the applicants who advanced to the third round are published on the Foundation’s website. Upon completion of the second round, no more than 40 (forty) candidates are selected. 3.6. The third round is an interview. 3.6.1. The third round is held from May 13 through May 23, 2024. 3.6.2. Participants of the third round are invited to an online interview with the Foundation’s Expert Council. During the interview process independent experts evaluate: a) analytical skills; b) competencies of candidates; с) programming skills and experience (if there are any); d) high intellectual potential; e) striving for continuous professional development; f) other 3.6.3. In the course of the interview, the quality of the contest applicants will also be assessed through observation of the following areas: a. general communication; b. system of values and attitudes, motivation; c. general activity; d. emotional intellect; e. understanding the demands of the modern world; f. psychological maturity; g. thinking; h. the applicants’ willingness to make motivational posts for social media. 3.6.4. Duration of the interview is up to 40 (forty) minutes. Upon

completion of the third round of the contest, 20 (twenty) winners are selected. 3.6.5. The Foundation reserves the right not to comment on its decisions to the participants of the Contest. 3.6.6. The Foundation makes a final decision until May 24, 2024. The list of the contest winners is published on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org. IV. Sponsorship Procedure. 4.1. 20 (twenty) winners from among the best participants of the Program’s contest get sponsorship. The number of the Program winners may be increased or decreased depending on the quantity and quality of the applications received and the decision of the Foundation’s Expert Council. 4.2. Through a competitive selection process of the Program, the winners undergo free 4-week training at the Summer school of the Yessenov Data Lab Foundation (hereinafter referred to as “the School”) from June 17 through July 13, 2024, including 3 (three) weeks online - from June 17 through July 05, 2024, and 1 (one) week offline - from July 08 through July 13, 2024, in Almaty. The Foundation covers travel and accommodation costs for those winners who come to Almaty from other regions. The Foundation provides meals for all the

School participants during the offline training. 4.4. Before commencement of the training at the School, the Foundation and the winner of the Program sign a sponsorship agreement (hereinafter referred to as “the Agreement’). 4.5. The winner of the Program who has received a training grant is obliged to comply with the terms of the Sponsorship Agreement with the Foundation, including, but not limited to: 1) attend the School classes according to the schedule and not to miss classes without good reason; 3 2) perform а! the challenging tasks, including tasks for independent work as part of the training а the School; 3) take their exams at the School timely; 4) comply with other obligations and rules of the School; 5) disseminate information about the Foundation in any acceptable form, including publications on social media, ensuring a positive reputation of the Foundation, as well as distribute information and advertising materials provided by the Foundation; 6) bring information to the Foundation about personal achievements in professional activity and soft skills; 7) participate in filming, information sessions and other communication activities as and when requested by the

Foundation. These photos and videos can be used in future in social media and publications of the Foundation; 8) provide information as and when requested by the Foundation no later than 3 (three) business days from the date of receipt of the request by email specified in the application; 9) as and when requested by the Foundation, provide assistance in its activities and programs of the Foundation; 10) as and when requested by the Foundation, provide personal photos, textual materials and videos (from the place of study/work/internship/social events, etc.) (the appearance and clothing of people in the photos and videos must be neat and secular), as well as participate in photo and video sessions during the Foundation events, in order to create success stories on the Foundation’s information resources and other publications which can motivate the youth of Kazakhstan; 11) in performing the obligations under the Agreement, the winner of the Program and employees of the Foundation refrain from committing, inducing to commit actions that violate or contribute to the violation of the legislation of the Republic of Kazakhstan, including in the field of combating corruption, do not pay,

PROVISIONS of the Yessenov Data Lab Program |. General terms. 1.1 The Yessenov Data Lab Program (hereinafter referred to as the “Program’) is a 4-week intensive training course for young Kazakhstani scientists and professionals in the basic skills of working with Big Data and artificial intelligence. Participants will acquire knowledge and practical skills that will help them apply advanced technologies in their professional activities. 1.2. The Program is funded by the initiative of the private “Тһе Shakhmardan Yessenov Science and Education Foundation” (hereinafter referred to as “the Foundation’). 1.3. The Program is aimed to support the acquisition of skills in working with Big Data and artificial intelligence by young scientists, which are essential for further professional growth and independent work in this field. 1.4. In these Provisions, the term “sponsorship” refers to funding for training, including payment of lecturer fees, as well as technical and administrative costs. 1.5. Participation in the Program is free of charge for the winners. Twenty (20) Program winners are selected from among the Program competitors annually. 1.6. The winners of the Program are determined

in an open contest. The contest is held once a year. 1.7. The Program involves training in Big Data and artificial intelligence skills for four (4) weeks from June 16 through July 11, 2025, at the Yessenov Data Lab Summer School of the Foundation. This includes two (2) weeks online - from June 16 through June 27, 2025, and two (2) weeks offline - from June 30 through July 11, 2025, in Almaty. Language of instruction is Russian, with the possible use of technical English elements. |. Requirements for the Program participants. 2.1. Only citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan who are over eighteen (18) years of age, undergraduate students, master’s students, doctoral students and working professionals in the following areas* can participate in the Program: - natural sciences, mathematics and statistics; - information and communication technologies; - engineering, manufacturing and construction industries; - healthcare. * according to the Classifier of Training Programs for Personnel with Higher and Post-Graduate Education (Approved by the Order of the Minister of Education and Science of the Republic of Kazakhstan as of October 13, 2018, No. 569). 2.2. The following qualities of

applicants will be taken into consideration when determining the winner of the Program: 2.2.1. high intellectual potential; 2.2.2. willingness to develop professional skills; 2.2.3. focusing on results; 2.2.4. adaptability. 2.5. The applicants must declare and provide documentation of their active professional, scientific, social activities, as well as demonstrate that their professional skills meet the following requirements: 2.3.1. strong analytical skills; 2.3.2. interest and willingness to engage in scientific activities and/or develop IT technologies in Kazakhstan; 2.5.5. basic knowledge of statistics and linear algebra; 2.3.4. basic knowledge of programming (in any programming language). 2.4. The applicant must have a personal laptop with characteristics sufficient for comfortable programming. Recommended parameters: 2.4.1. CPU Intel Core Gen 7 and higher, Mac Silicon M1 and higher. 2.4.2. RAM 6 GB and higher. 2.5. To have a stable Internet connection with a recommended speed of 20 Mbps, but not less than 10 Mbps, or higher. 2.6 Тһе following will be an advantage: 2.6.1 interest in science; 2.6.2 own scientific papers, materials, results of any research, etc.; 2.6.3 B1 or

higher proficiency in English. lll. Stages of competitive selection process of the Program. 3.1. The competition implies three (3) selection stages, at the end of each of which the best applicants are selected. The announcement about the opening of the competition is published on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org and on the Foundation’s social media. 3.2. An Expert Council is created at the Foundation to conduct the competitive selection among the participants of the Program. The decision of the Expert Council is submitted to the Head of the Foundation for consideration. 5.5. The decision of the Head of the Foundation is approved by the order which contains officially certified list of the Program winners. The list of the Program winners is published on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org 5.4. The first round is acceptance and selection of applications. To participate in the selection process for the Program, the candidate must: 3.4.1. fill out the online application on the Foundation's website: yessenovfoundation.org; 3.4.2. attach to the application: Mandatory documents: 1. copy of ID; 2. student transcript (GPA not lower than 3.0). Applications with a

GPA lower than 3.0 will not be considered. 3. certificate of no criminal record/presence of a criminal record. 4. a copy of the transcript (all semesters) or undergraduate diploma with grades (for undergraduates and professionals); 5. an essay on the topic "| want to learn how to analyze data so that...” (not less than 7 sentences and no more than 750 words) must be submitted only in an editable Microsoft Word document and written in the language of the application form — Kazakh, Russian, or English. 6.CV Additional documents: 1. a portfolio demonstrating the candidate’s achievements in scientific research and/or expressed interests in a specific scientific/technical area, along with providing relevant supporting documents: a. certificates for participation/victories in an olympiad — no more than two (2) certificates*; Б. certificates confirming completion of courses, etc. — no more than two (2) certificates”; с. certificates for social activity and volunteering — no more than two (2) certificates*; а. publications — no more than two (2) publications”; e. links to projects in which the candidate participated or implemented independently - no more than 3 examples (for each example,

no more than 2 photos/screenshots of pages/links to websites and/or social media, along with a text explanation of no more than 150 words)*. *When reviewing additional documents, the Foundation considers the limited number of certificates/publications provided by the candidate. The candidate must independently select the certificates/publications/others that best represent personal achievements. 3.4.3. Applications are accepted on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org, from March 31 through May 5, 2025. The deadline for acceptance of applications is May 5, 2025, 3:00 P.M. Astana time (GMT+5). Applications received after the deadline will not be considered. 3.4.4. An application and essay сап be submitted in the Kazakh, Russian, or English languages. Documents (certificates, diplomas, recommendations, etc.) can be provided in their original languages. 3.4.5. Submission documents to the competition means that the applicant shares the goals of the Program and agrees with all the terms of the competition’s procedure set out in these Provisions and is ready to fully comply with them. 3.4.6. All the applications received on time are reviewed and assessed by the Foundation

employees and independent experts by May 19, 2025, inclusive. 3.4.7. Lists of the applicants who advanced to the second round will be published по later than Мау 20, 2025, on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org. Upon completion of the first round of the competition, no more than sixty (60) candidates will be selected. 3.5. The second round is testing. 3.5.1. The second round is held from May 21 through Мау 25, 2025. 3.5.1 The Maths and Logic test. The test consists of thirty (30) questions and lasts 60 minutes. It is held online; 5.5.2. Data analysis exam. The exam is a regression problem. 5.5.5. For a successful passing the test, the candidate must have basic knowledge of the following topics: 1) matrices, linear equations; 2) graphs (mathematics); 3) linear regression, means, medians; 4) graphing functions; 5) variables, loops, conditions, arrays (in any programming language); 6) basic knowledge of statistics and probability theory - probability distributions, random variable, math. expectation, covariance, normal distribution. 5.5.4. Lists of the applicants who advanced to the third round will be published no later than May 26, 2025, on the Foundation’s website.

Upon completion of the second round, no more than forty (40) candidates will be selected. 3.6. The third round is an interview. 3.6.1. The third round is held from May 27 through June 5, 2025. 3.6.2. Participants of the third round are invited to an online interview with the Foundation’s Expert Council. During the interview process independent experts evaluate: a. analytical skills; b. professional qualities of the candidates; с. programming skills and experience (if there are any); d. high intellectual potential; e. striving for continuous professional development; f. other 5.6.5. During the interview, the experts will also assess candidates based on the following criteria: a. general communication; b. value system and attitudes, motivation; c. general activity; d. emotional intelligence; e. understanding of the demands of the modern world; f. general psychological maturity; g. thinking; h. willingness of the applicants to post motivational content on social media. 3.6.4. Duration of the interview is up to forty (40) minutes. Upon completion of the third round of the competition, twenty (20) winners will be selected. 5.6.5. The Foundation reserves the right not to comment on its

decisions to the participants of the competition. 3.6.6. The list of the winners of the competition will be published no later than June 6, 2025, on the Foundation’s website: yessenovfoundation.org. №. Sponsorship Procedure. 4.1. Sponsorship assistance is granted to twenty (20) winners from among the best participants of the Program's competition. The number of the Program winners may be increased or decreased depending on the quantity and quality of the applications received, and the decision of the Foundation’s Expert Council. 4.2. As a result of the competitive selection process of the Program, the winners will undergo a 4-week training at the Foundation’s Yessenov Data Lab Summer School (hereinafter referred to as “the School”) free of charge, from June 16 through July 11, 2025, including two (2) weeks online - from June 16 through June 27, 2025, and two (2) weeks offline - from June 30 through July 11, 2025, in Almaty. The Foundation covers travel and accommodation expenses for the winners residing outside of Almaty. The Foundation provides meals for all participants of the School during the offline training in Almaty. 4.3. Before commencement of the training at the School,

the Foundation and the winner of the Program sign a Sponsorship Agreement (hereinafter referred to as “the Agreement”). 4.4. Тре winner of the Program, who has received the grant for training, is required to comply with the terms of the Agreement with the Foundation, including but not limited to the following: 1) attend the School classes according to the schedule and not miss classes without a valid reason; 2) responsibly complete all assignments, including independent work tasks as part of the training at the School. The completion of practical tasks will be checked using artificial intelligence systems that analyze the quality and completeness of the work; 3) pass the exams at the School timely; 4) comply with other obligations and rules of the School; 5) disseminate information about the Foundation in any acceptable form, including posts on personal social media accounts that promote a positive reputation for the Foundation, as well as distribute information and advertising materials provided by the Foundation. There must be at least 1 post on Instagram, Facebook, or LinkedIn from the end of the competition until one week after the training is completed. 6) provide the

Foundation with information about personal achievements in professional activities and soft skills; 7) participate in filming, information sessions and other communication activities as and when requested by the Foundation. These photos and videos may be used in the future on the Foundation’s social media and in its publications; 8) provide information as and when requested by the Foundation, no later than three (3) business days from the date of receipt of the request sent to the email specified in the application; 9) as and when requested by the Foundation, provide assistance in its activities and programs; 10) as and when requested by the Foundation, provide personal photos, textual materials and videos (from the places of study/work/internship/social events, etc.) (the appearance апа clothing of individuals in the photos and videos must be neat and secular), as well as participate in photo and video sessions during the Foundation events, in order to create success stories for the Foundation’s information resources and other publications, aiming to motivate the youth of Kazakhstan; 11) in performing the obligations under the Agreement, the winner of the Program and employees of

the Foundation refrain from committing, inducing to commit actions that violate or contribute to the violation of the legislation of the Republic of Kazakhstan, including in the field of combating corruption, do not pay, do not offer to pay and do not authorize disbursement of any money or valuables, directly or indirectly, to any individuals, in order to influence the actions or decisions of such individuals to obtain any improper advantage or achieve any other improper purpose. 4.5. ПОЛОЖЕНИЕ О ПРЕДОСТАВЛЕНИИ СПОНСОРСКОЙ ПОМОЩИ НА ОБУЧЕНИЕ В МАГИСТРАТУРЕ ПО ЕСТЕСТВЕННО-НАУЧНЫМ И ТЕХНИЧЕСКИМ НАПРАВЛЕНИЯМ ОБЩИЕ ПОЛОЖЕНИЯ Программа предоставления спонсорской помощи научно-образовательного фонда им. Академика Шахмардана Есенова (именуемый в дальнейшем Фонд) молодым ученым для обучения в магистратуре по естественно-научным и техническим специальностям направлена на поддержку талантливых казахстанцев, занимающихся научной деятельностью, а также на развитие научного и исследовательского потенциала страны в целом. В данном Положении смыслом термина «Спонсорская помощь» является денежная выплата, предоставляемая участникам программы в целях обучения в магистратуре ведущих вузов мира по естественно-научным и техническим направлениям Фонд осуществляет данную программу в рамках понимания того, что обучение в ведущих вузах по магистерским программам дает молодым талантливым ученым Казахстана возможность расширения своих знаний и применения новых технологий в проведении исследований, международного обмена опытом в научной сфере, развития профессиональных компетенций на новом уровне и др. Развитие

данной программы является одним из направлений деятельности Фонда по формированию отечественных научных кадров. Программа проводится и финансируется по инициативе научно-образовательного фонда им. Академика Шахмардана Есенова. Участниками Программы являются молодые ученые, занимающиеся исследованиями по техническим и естественно-научным направлениям и являющиеся гражданами РК, которые приглашены на обучение по магистерской программе в ведущий вуз. Обучение по магистерской программе должно стать логическим продолжением карьеры Соискателя в научной деятельности по определенному направлению. КОНКУРСНЫЙ ОТБОР УЧАСТНИКОВ ПРОГРАММЫ Требования к участникам Программы 2.1.1. Участниками программы могут быть граждане РК старше 18 лет студенты 4 курса или действующие молодые ученые со степенью не выше бакалавра и окончившие вуз в последние 5 лет (на момент подачи заявки), занимаюшиеся исследованиями по техническим и естественно-научным направлениям которые приглашены на обучение ПО магистерской программе в ведуший вуз по своему научному направлению; 2.1.2. Соискатель должен заявить и подтвердить документально свою активную профессиональную, научную, общественную деятельность: 2.1.3.

Соискатели должны обучаться на бакалавриате или иметь соответствующий диплом бакалавра по естественно-научному или техническому направлению с уровнем СРА не ниже 3,3 балла. Данные должны быть подтверждены официальным транскриптом. 2.1.4. Соискатели должны иметь достижения в олимпиадах и/или опыт работы над научными проектами в период обучения на бакалавриате. Данные должны быть подтверждены копиями грамот/ сертификатов и рекомендацией научного руководителя, собственными научными статьями, результатами каких-либо исследований; 2.1.5. Соискатель должен знать английский язык не ниже уровня upper intermediate.
